questions.question: Four words are given in which only one is correctly spelt. Find the correctly spelt word.
questions.explanation: Inveterate means habitual. There are four syllables in inveterate: in-vet-er-ate. Example: Sonakshi is an inveterate reader who always has a book in her hands.

questions.question: Four words are given in which only one is correctly spelt. Find the correctly spelt word.
questions.explanation: Repertoire means the entire range of skills or aptitudes or devices used in a particular field or occupation. There are three syllables in repertoire: rep-er-toire. Example: In order to do a programming job, I had to increase my repertoire of coding skills.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the alternative which can best substitute the given phrase. A very large area of sea surrounded on three sides by a coast
questions.explanation: Gulf: A very large area of sea surrounded on three sides by a coast. Peninsula: a piece of land almost surrounded by water or projecting out into a body of water Island: A piece of land surrounded by water. Terrain: A stretch of land especially with regard to its physical features.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the alternative which can best substitute the given phrase. One who is concerned only with the making of money, even at the expense of ethics
questions.explanation: Barbarian: Uncivilized; one who has no manners. Imposter: a person who pretends to be someone else in order to deceive others, especially for fraudulent gain. Insolvent: One who has to pay no debts. Mercenary: One who is concerned only with the making of money, even at the expense of ethics

questions.question: Where did the flock of the sheep follow the bellwether into?
questions.explanation: The answer is given in the 3ʳᵈ to last sentence of the passage - "And when they had wheeled round their leader for a time, they ran off after him with a bound into the wood." Hence, option B will be the correct answer.

questions.question: Why the little Bo-Peep had to rise early in the morning?
options: She had to take the sheep grazing.
She had to feed the flock.
She had to go to the river.
questions.explanation: Although the context of the question is given in the very 1ˢᵗ sentence of the passage, it has been nowhere mentioned why she had to rise early in the morning. Hence, option D will be the correct answer.

questions.question: What was the setting of the story?
questions.explanation: Given in the very first sentence of the passage - "One summer evening, when the sun was setting, Little Bo-Peep, who had to rise very early in the morning, felt tired, and sat down on a bank covered with daisies," option A will be the correct answer.

questions.question: What was the name of the flower with which the bank was covered?
questions.explanation: Given in the very first sentence of the passage - "One summer evening, when the sun was setting, Little Bo-Peep, who had to rise very early in the morning, felt tired, and sat down on a bank covered with daisies," option A will be the correct answer.

questions.question: What happened after the bellwether whirled himself round like a top and shook his head and rang his bell?
questions.explanation: The answer is given in the 4ᵗʰ to last sentence of the given passage - "Very soon the rest of the flock began to dance and caper too." Hence, option C will be the correct answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, four sentences are given, which when properly arranged, make up a paragraph. Rearrange the given sentences and choose the option that provides the correct final order. P. When I started blogging, I would feel so awkward sharing my posts with people I knew and even sharing it from my personal Facebook profile. Q. Selling yourself is one of the most awkward things you'll probably ever do. R. It definitely gets easier with practice and can sometimes even be fun. S. But if you want to make a positive impact and grow your brand, it's just something you'll have to learn to do.
questions.explanation: Q begins the passage by establishing the subject - the awkwardness of selling yourself. P talks about awkwardness specifically with respect to the author. Therefore, QP is a pair. S seems to provide a solution to overcome the awkwardness. PS is a pair. R concludes the passage. The correct sequence by joining all the above-mentioned points would be QPSR. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, four sentences are given, which when properly arranged, make up a paragraph. Rearrange the given sentences and choose the option that provides the correct final order. P. I usually meditate and workout in the morning because that really ups my energy level and helps me have a more productive day. Q. So be more mindful of how you're spending your mornings. R. What you do in the morning sets the tone for the rest of your day. S. I also plan my day in the morning, so I know what work I need to get done that day.
questions.explanation: R is kind of a generic statement and Q is the advice related to the statement R. RQ is a pair. PS follow as a pair: P talks about how the author follows his own advice, and S builds further on it. Both P and R can be the opening statement but the option with P as the opening statement does not have PS as a pair. Option (a) is eliminated. Similarly, RQ as a pair is not present in options (b) and (c) and therefore eliminated. Hence, (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, a sentence is given in either direct or indirect speech. Select the option that correctly converts the sentence in direct speech to indirect speech or vice versa. "I was chased by the police and nearly got hit by a car," said Samarth.
options: Samarth said that he was chased by the police and nearly got hit by a car.
Samarth said that he is chased by the police and nearly gets hit by a car.
Samarth said that he had been chased by the police and nearly got hit by a car.
Samarth said that he has been chased by the police and nearly got hit by a car.
questions.explanation: When converting direct to indirect speech, the simple past tense (was chased, got hit) will change to the past perfect (had been chased, got hit). A is incorrect as it does not change the tense. B is incorrect as 'is chased' and 'gets hit' is in simple present tense. D is incorrect as 'has been chased' is in present perfect tense. C is the right answer as it uses the past perfect tense.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, select the word similar in meaning to the word given. Abscond
questions.explanation: 'Abscond' means to leave hurriedly and secretly. The closest synonym would be to 'disappear', making option D, the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, select the word similar in meaning to the word given. Extrapolate
questions.explanation: 'Extrapolate' means to guess or think about what might happen using information that is already known. Hence, option A will be the correct answer. 'Perimeter' means the outermost parts or boundary of an area or object.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the word opposite in meaning to the word given. Lopsided
questions.explanation: 'Lopsided' means with one side lower or smaller than the other. 'Asymmetrical' is its synonym, meaning that 'symmetrical' (made up of exactly similar parts facing each other or around an axis) will be the antonym. Option C will be the correct answer. 'Lineate' means having or marked with lines.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the word opposite in meaning to the word given. Asinine
questions.explanation: 'Asinine' = 'inane' = extremely stupid or foolish. The most obvious opposite would be 'intelligent' , making option B the correct answer. 'Aloof' means not friendly or forthcoming; cool and distant.

questions.question: In the below question, a sentence has been given in Active/Passive Voice. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Passive/Active Voice. The loud clamor that followed this impious proposal, distracted my soul with an excess of anguish.
options: My soul had been distracted with an excess of anguish by the loud clamor that followed this impious proposal.
My soul was distracted by an excess of anguish with the loud clamor that followed this impious proposal.
My soul was distracted by the loud clamor that followed this impious proposal with an excess of anguish.
My soul was distracted with an excess of anguish by the loud clamor that followed this impious proposal.
questions.explanation: Option D is the right answer. Option A is eliminated as it changes the tense used in the original sentence. Option B is eliminated as it changes the meaning of the sentence by switching the use of the prepositions. Option C is eliminated as it is incongruous and ambiguous due to the change in the order of clauses.

questions.question: Four alternatives are given for the bold idiom/phrase. Choose the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the idiom/phrase. Queer fish
questions.explanation: The idiom means a person whose behavior is strange.

questions.question: Four alternatives are given for the bold idiom/phrase. Choose the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the idiom/phrase. A great hand
questions.explanation: The idiom means to be an expert at something.

questions.question: A sentence/a part of the sentence is underlined. Four alternatives are given to the underlined part which will improve the sentence. Choose the correct alternative and click the button corresponding to it. In case no improvement is needed, click the button corresponding to "No improvement". India has rebuked of American lack of sympathy for its concerns with Pakistan's policy.
questions.explanation: 'Rebuked' means to scold someone, which does not fit here. Moreover, it is grammatically incorrect, to use 'of' after 'rebuked'. The construction 'had + verb' must have the verb in past tense. So A is wrong. 'Unhappy' is an adjective; we need a verb. B fits the sentence correctly.

questions.question: A sentence/a part of the sentence is underlined. Four alternatives are given to the underlined part which will improve the sentence. Choose the correct alternative and click the button corresponding to it. In case no improvement is needed, click the button corresponding to "No improvement". Please recommend the doctor before you take any medicine.
questions.explanation: 'Recommend' means to suggest - this is inappropriate here, as the doctor is the one to recommend something, not the patient. 'Discuss' and 'talk' would only be correct if followed by the preposition 'with'. So, A is the right answer, as 'consult' means to ask.

questions.question: Some parts of the sentence have errors and some are correct. Find out which part of a sentence has an error choose the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, choose "No Error" option. My teacher said to me that (1)/ all that (2)/ glittered was not gold(3)./ No Error(4)
questions.explanation: The correct sentence is "My teacher said to me that all that glitters is not gold". If a universal truth or proverb is stated in reported speech, it should not be changed even if the reporting verb is in past tense.

questions.question: Some parts of the sentences have errors and some are correct. Find out which part of a sentence has an error. If a sentence is free from error, choose "No error" option. The Andes mountains (1)/ lay between (2)/ Chile and Argentina (3)./ No Error(4)
questions.explanation: The correct usage is "Lie" and not "Lay". "Lay" is a transitive verb. It requires a direct subject and one or more objects.

questions.question: Four alternatives A, B, C and D are given under each sentence, you are required to select the most suitable alternative to fill in the blank in the sentence to make it meaningful. Man power is the_______ means of converting other resources to mankind's use and benefit.
questions.explanation: Indispensable means essential, hence fits in the blank. D is the right answer. insuperable - (of a difficulty or obstacle) impossible to overcome. inimitable - unique.

questions.question: Four alternatives A, B, C and D are given under each sentence, you are required to select the most suitable alternative to fill in the blank in the sentence to make it meaningful. Rajeev failed in the examination because none of his answers were______ to the question asked.
questions.explanation: Pertinent means relating to the subject. allusive - Using or containing suggestion rather than explicit mention. referential - Containing or of the nature of references or allusions. Hence, C is the right answer.
